router2 OSPF process is otherwise active (other adjacency to router3 on Serial0/1/1 is full), so the issue is localized to the Serial0/1/0 receive path (get_ospf: router2 shows neighbor on Serial0/1/1 but none on Serial0/1/0)

plan_history.intent_description: R2 has a one-sided OSPF adjacency on Serial0/1/0 (R2 not receiving Router1 hellos), causing the OSPF neighbor on that link to remain down. Restore bidirectional OSPF adjacency so routes exchange on that link.
plan_history.problem: Router2 Serial0/1/0 is not receiving OSPF Hello packets from Router1 (one-way adjacency), preventing OSPF adjacency formation on that interface.
plan_history.fix_summary: Allow inbound OSPF packets to reach router2 on Serial0/1/0 by removing any inbound ACL or filter on that interface that blocks protocol 89, then clear OSPF neighbors on that interface to force reestablishment. Operational risk: temporarily removing an ACL may permit additional traffic until replaced/adjusted.

plan_history.intent_description: Serial0/1/0 on R2 is administratively down, preventing R2 from receiving OSPF hellos from R1 and leaving the OSPF adjacency down. Restore the interface to up so the OSPF adjacency can form and routes can be exchanged.
plan_history.problem: Interface Serial0/1/0 on router2 is administratively down causing the OSPF neighbor with R1 to remain down.
plan_history.fix_summary: Bring up router2 Serial0/1/0 to restore bidirectional OSPF adjacency with R1. Inbound ACL was already removed in a prior change.

remaining_issues: Bidirectional OSPF adjacency between R1 and R2 on the Serial0/1/0 link has not been verified (router1 still reported the interface as Down in the most recent OSPF snapshot).
Need confirmation that R2 is receiving OSPF hellos from R1 and that the neighbor relationship reached FULL on both sides.
